channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Trump retreats from shipping toll in Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw, Suzan Haidamous, Maegan Vazquez
item.description: As European markets fell and oil prices hit a one-month high, President Donald Trump declared on Truth Social that the strait “is open to ALL Ship traffic except for Iran.”

item.title: Russia hopes Lindsey Graham’s death will advance Kremlin interests
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: Graham had carried the reputation as the toughest Russia hawk in Washington since the 2018 death of his close friend and Senate ally John McCain.

item.title: Trump says U.S. military will reimpose blockade on Iranian ports
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Tara Copp, Gerry Shih
item.description: The president said the U.S. will impose a 20 percent fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz despite official assertions that fees would violate international law.

item.title: U.K. to designate Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a terrorist group
item.dc:creator: Greg Miller
item.description: U.K. authorities have linked the IRGC and its proxies to a wave of violent plots on British soil.

item.title: Vatican hits back at U.S. claim that Pope Leo speaks as a politician
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: President Donald Trump's ambassador had sought to portray the pontiff as the political leader of the Holy See. The Vatican swiftly said he is “proclaiming the Gospel.”

item.title: Fire at Bangkok bar kills 27; exits may have been blocked, officials say
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Tan, Huiyee Chiew
item.description: Preliminary investigations indicated that the fire may have been started by electrical short circuits, and that emergency exits may have been blocked.

item.title: Renovated Buckingham Palace will be a very royal office building
item.dc:creator: Karla Adam
item.description: Since Queen Victoria made it her official residence in 1837, every British sovereign has lived there. King Charles III, born at the palace, is the first to opt out.
